methodology: Phase 7 P3.2 first recon (Session 30). Ran sprite_slot_trace.lua with the default schedule 60,480,60,480,0 against launch_octopus_start.mss (David-authored, post-password, walked past the stage intro to the first distinct enemy spawn). Output at knowledge_base/mmx1/_phase7_runs/launch_octopus/recon. 3232 kind=1 rows across 4 distinct b12 codes (0x20, 0x5B, 0x4A at two b15 variants). All enemies share b11=0x01 — a NEW LO-class marker distinct from CP-start (b11=0x04) and CP mid-stage (b11=0x02, per Session 30's pre_capsule recon findings). b12 is the enemy-type code; b15 splits two b4A instances that may be variants or spawn-point markers.
notes_on_class_marker: b11=0x01 class uses OAM palette row 4 for rendered frog-type sprites. Session 30 user feedback (after iterating atlas decoder multiple times) established: pal 1 = X body, pal 2 = HUD/HP bar, pal 4 = the actual frog enemy (RayBit / Anglerge visual). Pal 7 was my initial guess for b20 but user confirmed pal 7 outlines 'nothing visible' in the recon — likely OAM slots with tile data not rendered or engine reserved. Pos-offset discovery (find_pos_per_archetype.py Phase 6 defaults) does NOT work on this class — slot position bytes b14/b15 decode to class constants rather than per-instance positions (KB-053 Finding B). sprites_ref_builder.py's --enemy-palette mode (Session 30) + X-exclusion + per-frame dominant cluster + far-from-X state-rep picker are all required to produce atlases free of X/HUD contamination (KB-054).

spawns.notes: Frog-type LO enemy (one of 2 visible instances in the recon alongside b5B). Appears in slot 3 at f76, migrates to slot 7 at f335 (dynamic slot reallocation). Full 5-state FSM observed. Mobile — state bboxes in final clean atlas span from x=25 (state 2) to x=258 (state 4). **Session 30 atlas pipeline corrections:** (1) initial pal 7 guess WRONG — user-confirmed pal=4 (pink outline on palette scan) = the frog. (2) Initial atlases mixed X's aux sprites and HUD font tiles because pal 4 is shared between frog + X's buster/charge FX. Fixed via X-exclusion (>50 px from X's pal-1 centroid) + farthest-from-X cluster picker + state-rep frame chosen only when frog visible far from X. Tile IDs now clean 64..119 enemy range. HP not yet verified — combat trace workflow pending.

Frog-type LO enemy (same visual archetype as b20, different b12 code — likely a second frog instance with its own state timing, possibly a variant subtype). Four-state FSM {0,2,4,6}: init -> state2 <-> state6 <-> state4 -> state2 (probabilistic branch at state4). Slot 5 primary, slot 11 second instance post-f443. Atlas pipeline corrected in Session 30 via X-exclusion + farthest-cluster + far-from-X state-rep (see b20 notes for full story). Final bboxes in clean atlas: state 2 x=4-28, state 4 x=0-22, state 6 x=2-18 — frog on the left side of screen across states. Tile IDs 96-143 in enemy-sprite range. HP not yet verified.

566-frame uninterrupted state=2 idle; pos bytes are null (b14=b15=b0C=0x00, b0D=0x6D); OAM palette budget saturated by X + BG/HUD + b20 + b5B without a 5th palette row for b4A sprites. Hypothesis: b4A is an invisible trigger / spawner entity rather than a rendered enemy. Written up in enemies/lo_enemy_b4A/sprite_status.md. Same b12 but two b15 values across 3 slot instances — may be two trigger variants. Needs a longer capture or a dedicated recon that crosses b4A's spawn points to confirm the trigger hypothesis (observe b4A toggling adjacent slots) or surface a 5th palette row.

omitted_slots.reason: No kind=1 rows observed in the recon window. Reserved or scene-specific.

queued_work: Combat traces for lo_enemy_b20 and lo_enemy_b5B to pin HP + verify state-4 semantics (fire / hurt / other) via OAM motion analysis.
Confirm or refute the lo_enemy_b4A invisible-trigger hypothesis via a longer capture or a b4A-targeted recon (settle near slot 9's spawn, walk slowly past with OAM dumps at higher frequency).

Find per-b11=0x01-class pos-offset encoding (KB-053 Finding B). Currently the --enemy-palette workaround sidesteps this, but knowing the real pos offsets would enable the default spatial-proximity filter to work for future LO captures without palette hand-off.

Re-run behavior_extractor once tall_shooter-style probabilistic_branch support is validated end-to-end on the LO FSMs — b20's state2 -> state4 / state6 branch (3 vs 4 instances) is close to the snow_shooter alt-fire pattern.
